title: Why IREN Stock Is Trading Higher Thursday?
text:
IREN Limited's stock surged following the settlement of its $2.6 billion convertible senior notes offering. The company expects net proceeds of approximately $2.57 billion after expenses, which will support general corporate purposes and working capital, including $174.5 million for capped call transactions.

Analyst sentiment remains mixed. Macquarie maintained an outperform rating with a raised price target of $90, while JPMorgan kept an underweight rating but increased its forecast to $46.

Short interest decreased from 59.14 million to 58.36 million shares, with shorts representing 16.87% of the float. Technical analysis shows the stock trading above major moving averages, with key resistance at $59 and support at $48.50.

At publication time, IREN shares were up 4.89% at $57.87.
